decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing creatures like Birds of Paradise and Wall of Roots to ramp up mana quickly. Survival of the Fittest allows for creature tutoring, while Goblin Welder and Genesis provide recursion and value. Tangle Wire and Tsabo's Web help to slow down opponents, while Triskelion and Goblin Sharpshooter provide efficient removal. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by generating card advantage with cards like Sylvan Library and outlasting them with resilient creatures like Deranged Hermit and Thopter Squadron. Gaea's Cradle and Karplusan Forest provide additional mana sources for a consistent game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through various counterspells and removal spells while utilizing Zombie Infestation as a win condition. The key concept is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with removal and counterspells, while creating a continuous stream of zombie tokens to overwhelm them. The deck also includes card draw to ensure consistency in drawing key pieces. Overall, the strategy revolves around utilizing control elements to maintain board presence and outvalue the opponent over time.

The deck focuses on using Hermit Druid to fill the graveyard with creatures, then using cards like Karmic Guide, Animate Dead, and Phyrexian Devourer to create infinite loops and win the game. It also includes other combo pieces such as Palinchron and Psychatog, along with disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to handle threats from the opponent. The mana base is designed to support the deck's multicolored requirements, with City of Brass and Gemstone Mine providing versatile mana sources. The strategy is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful interactions.

This deck aims to lock down opponent's resources and disrupt their game plan using Stasis and various bounce spells like Chain of Vapor and Boomerang. By restricting opponent's mana and card draws with Black Vise and countering their spells with Counterspell, the deck aims to set up a soft lock and eventually win with combos like Brain Freeze or Upheaval. The inclusion of Forsaken City provides an alternative mana source to keep the Stasis lock going, while cards like Gush and Impulse help with card advantage and consistency.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through the use of disruptive spells and creatures like Meddling Mage and Quirion Dryad. Psychatog serves as a finisher, growing in power as spells are played. The deck includes cards that allow for card selection and manipulation, such as Gush and Sleight of Hand. Armageddon is included to disrupt opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to control the board, disrupt opponent's strategy, and ultimately win through the power of Psychatog.

This deck focuses on aggressively utilizing creatures with madness and threshold abilities to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By discarding cards with madness, creatures can be cast at a reduced cost, allowing for explosive early-game plays. Additionally, achieving threshold enables powerful synergies and boosts the effectiveness of certain cards in the deck. The inclusion of removal spells and utility lands provides versatility and control options while maintaining a proactive game plan. Overall, this deck aims to pressure the opponent with efficient threats and capitalize on synergistic interactions to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through countering spells, removing threats, and generating card advantage. The deck aims to stall the game using cards like Standstill and control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God. It utilizes lands as a resource, with cards like Dust Bowl and Mishra's Factory to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck's win condition is to eventually overwhelm the opponent with flyers from Faerie Conclave and finish the game with Decree of Justice or through chip damage from Mishra's Factory.

The focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's hand and board presence through discard spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, while utilizing efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Ravenous Rats to pressure the opponent. The Rack provides additional damage as the opponent is forced to discard cards, and cards like Cursed Scroll and Funeral Charm offer versatile removal options. Mishra's Factory and Wasteland allow for some land disruption, while maintaining a strong presence on the battlefield. Overall, the deck aims to gain control and outvalue the opponent in the long game.

The main concept of this Deadguy Ale deck is to aggressively control and disrupt your opponent's strategy while also building up card advantage with cards like Phyrexian Arena and Gerrard's Verdict. By utilizing efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, along with hand disruption from Duress and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base, this deck aims to stay ahead on board presence and card advantage to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful engine of drawing cards and mana ramping through enchantment plays. By utilizing cards like Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence, the player can draw through their deck efficiently. Cards such as Wild Growth and Exploration help to accelerate their mana production. The deck also includes key combo pieces like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to control the board and finish the game. The main win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of powerful enchantments while controlling the game with removal and protective enchantments.

The main concept of this deck is to use enchantress cards like Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence to draw a large number of cards while ramping up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum. The deck also utilizes cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to create powerful board states and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Replenish allows the player to return enchantments from the graveyard to play, while cards like Seal of Cleansing and Sterling Grove offer removal and protection. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of card draw, mana ramp, and powerful enchantments.

The main focus of this deck is utilizing Goblin creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive tactics. With key cards like Goblin Lackey, Goblin Matron, and Goblin Piledriver, the deck aims to summon powerful creatures onto the battlefield rapidly. Supporting cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander provide additional removal and damage options. The inclusion of utility cards like Skirk Prospector and Wasteland help maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around fast-paced aggression and efficient creature management to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play while controlling the board with various removal spells and disruptive cards like Cataclysm and Rishadan Port. The deck also includes ways to draw cards and generate mana efficiently. The strategy revolves around quickly ramping into Terravore or other impactful creatures using Oath of Druids, while keeping the opponent in check with removal and disruption spells. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats while disrupting their game plan.

The deck focuses on using the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo with Stifle to quickly bring out a powerful creature. It also includes various counterspells and card draw spells to control the game and ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition efficiently. Additionally, it utilizes cards like Powder Keg and Phyrexian Furnace for additional utility and removal options. The goal is to outmaneuver and outpace the opponent with a well-timed combo that can win the game in a single turn.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Terravore while disrupting the opponent's resources with land destruction spells like Armageddon. Weathered Wayfarer helps to consistently hit land drops and maintain board presence. The deck aims to control the board early on and eventually overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. The inclusion of cards like Mox Diamond and Sylvan Library helps to accelerate the deck and maintain card advantage. Overall, this deck aims to apply early pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of Madness and Threshold mechanics. By using cards like Nimble Mongoose and Werebear, it aims to quickly fill up the graveyard to enable powerful Threshold abilities. Meanwhile, Meddling Mage disrupts the opponent's strategy, while cards like Swords to Plowshares and Call of the Herd provide removal and creature tokens for additional pressure. The deck also includes some card draw options like Accumulated Knowledge and Deep Analysis to maintain hand advantage. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with quick creature plays and disruption.
